name: competitive-landscape
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "analyze
capabilities: Apply Porter's Five Forces to assess industry attractiveness
Create competitive positioning maps to identify market gaps
Use Blue Ocean Strategy to find uncontested market space
Develop differentiation strategies across product, service, brand, and price
Build competitor profiles with strengths, weaknesses, and strategies
Establish competitive monitoring cadence for ongoing intelligence
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides business strategy frameworks for competitive landscape analysis. All static findings (14 external_commands, 1 weak cryptographic algorithm, 1 system reconnaissance) are false positives. The detected patterns are markdown text, ASCII diagrams, and business methodology content - no executable code exists. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: This skill helps users analyze their competitive environment using proven business frameworks. It provides structured methodologies like Porter's Five Forces and Blue Ocean Strategy to identify market positioning opportunities.
limitations: Does not provide real-time competitor data or pricing
Cannot access external company databases or private information
Requires user to provide specific competitor and market information
Framework output depends on quality of user-provided inputs

useCases.title: Startup Market Entry Analysis
useCases.description: New startup founders use this skill to understand competitive dynamics before launching a product, identifying blue ocean opportunities and defensible market positions.

useCases.title: Enterprise Competitive Review
useCases.description: Business analysts conduct quarterly competitive reviews for established companies, mapping competitor positions and identifying strategic threats.

useCases.title: Investment Due Diligence
useCases.description: Investors evaluate market attractiveness and competitive intensity for investment decisions, assessing industry forces and competitive dynamics.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Competitive Analysis Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to analyze the competitive environment for [industry/market]. Apply Porter's Five Forces to assess industry attractiveness and identify key competitive forces.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick competitive landscape overview

promptTemplates.title: Positioning Map Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to create a positioning map for [industry]. Plot competitors on [dimension 1: price vs. features] and [dimension 2: simplicity vs. complexity]. Identify white space opportunities.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to visualize competitive positions

promptTemplates.title: Differentiation Strategy Development
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to help develop a differentiation strategy for [company/product]. Consider product, service, brand, and price differentiation. Identify sustainable competitive advantages.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to define unique market positioning

promptTemplates.title: Blue Ocean Strategy Workshop
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to apply the Four Actions Framework (Eliminate, Reduce, Raise, Create) for our [product/service]. Identify opportunities to create uncontested market space.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want to find new market opportunities

bestPractices: Gather specific competitor data before analysis to ensure accurate framework application
Use multiple frameworks (Porter's, Blue Ocean, positioning maps) for comprehensive view
Validate findings with real customer interviews and market research
antiPatterns: Applying frameworks without understanding your specific market context
Using generic competitor lists instead of direct competitors
Ignoring substitute products and alternative solutions

faq.question: What is Porter's Five Forces?
faq.answer: Porter's Five Forces is a strategic framework that analyzes five key forces affecting industry attractiveness: threat of new entrants, bargaining power of suppliers, bargaining power of buyers, threat of substitutes, and competitive rivalry.

faq.question: How is this skill different from a market research tool?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured frameworks and methodologies for analysis. You provide the competitor and market data; the skill applies proven strategic frameworks to help you organize and interpret that information.

faq.question: Can this skill find competitors for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires you to identify and provide competitor information. It applies analysis frameworks to the data you provide rather than gathering data itself.

faq.question: What is Blue Ocean Strategy?
faq.answer: Blue Ocean Strategy is a methodology for creating uncontested market space by eliminating, reducing, raising, and creating factors in your industry. It helps find market opportunities where competition is irrelevant.

faq.question: How often should I update my competitive analysis?
faq.answer: Conduct deep competitive reviews quarterly, with weekly monitoring of competitor news and product changes. Reassess major strategic assumptions annually.

faq.question: Does this work for B2B and B2C markets?
faq.answer: Yes, the frameworks apply to both B2B and B2C markets. You may need to adjust dimensions for positioning maps and consider different buyer behavior patterns.
